food_subgroup: Fast food chicken wrap meal with sides

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic chicken wrap entry (FOODON_00002473); no specific McWrap or upsized meal composite entry exists. Closest parent match used for the wrap component; fries and beverage components are not separately coded here.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken sandwich or wrap, fast food
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers fast food chicken sandwiches and wraps. No specific McWrap composite meal code exists in FoodEx2; this is the closest defensible category anchor. The upsized meal includes large fries and a beverage which are not separately coded in this record.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for a fast food chicken wrap meal composite. SNOMED CT does not maintain granular fast food product codes.

serving.description: 1 upsized meal (McWrap + large fries + medium drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One upsized meal consisting of one Spicy Chicken McWrap (approximately 220 g), one large portion of french fries (approximately 154 g), and one medium soft drink (approximately 276 g / 250 ml), totalling approximately 650 g.

nutrient_source.title: McDonald's Singapore Nutrition Information — Spicy Chicken McWrap, Large Fries, Medium Soft Drink; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for fried chicken wrap components and french fries
nutrient_source.organization: McDonald's Singapore (primary product disclosure) / USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (secondary cross-reference)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: McDonald's Singapore publishes per-item nutrition data. The upsized meal is a composite of three items: Spicy Chicken McWrap (~220 g, ~430 kcal), Large Fries (~154 g, ~490 kcal), and a Medium Coke (~250 ml, ~110 kcal). Per-100g values are derived by summing the three components and dividing by total weight (650 g). USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for 'fast food chicken wrap' (NDB 21299) and 'fast food french fries' (NDB 21138) were used to cross-check macro ratios and secondary nutrient values where McDonald's Singapore does not publish full micronutrient panels. Sodium and saturated fat values are taken directly from McDonald's Singapore published data. This is an adapted composite estimate; confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; University of Sydney GI Database entries for white wheat flour tortilla wrap, french fries, and cola soft drink
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the McDonald's Spicy Chicken McWrap Upsized Meal as a composite. GI is estimated as a weighted average of the three meal components using carbohydrate contribution weighting: (1) Flour tortilla wrap — GI ~70 (University of Sydney GI Database, white wheat tortilla); (2) Large french fries — GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database, french fries, fast food); (3) Cola soft drink — GI ~63 (University of Sydney GI Database, Coca-Cola). Weighted composite GI calculated across total available carbohydrate (approximately 133 g per meal): wrap contributes ~35 g CHO at GI 70, fries contribute ~63 g CHO at GI 63, cola contributes ~35 g CHO at GI 63. Weighted GI = ((35×70)+(63×63)+(35×63))/133 ≈ 65. GL per serving = (65 × 133) / 100 = 86.7. This is a medium GI meal. Estimation basis documented per Veritas GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~2015 mg per meal serving)
hypertension.guidance: The upsized meal delivers approximately 2015 mg of sodium per serving, exceeding the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg in a single meal; individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of this meal and choose lower-sodium alternatives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~86.7 per meal) and high refined carbohydrate content from fries, tortilla, and cola
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic load of approximately 86.7 per meal and over 130 g of rapidly digestible carbohydrates from refined flour, fried potato, and sugar-sweetened cola, this meal is likely to cause a significant postprandial blood glucose spike and is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes without substantial dietary modification.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and total caloric density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This meal contains elevated saturated fat (~11.7 g per meal), detectable trans fat (~0.65 g per meal), and very high sodium (~2015 mg per meal), all of which are established risk factors for cardiovascular disease; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of this meal.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Chicken McWrap Upsized Meal is a McDonald's Singapore fast food combo meal consisting of three components: a Spicy Chicken McWrap, a large portion of french fries, and a medium beverage (typically a cola soft drink). The McWrap itself features a deep-fried spicy crispy chicken fillet wrapped in a soft flour tortilla with fresh shredded lettuce and creamy mayonnaise. The chicken fillet is seasoned with a spicy coating and deep-fried to a crisp texture, providing a contrast of crunchy exterior and juicy interior. The wrap is soft and pliable, with a mild wheat flavour. The large fries are salted and crispy. The meal is served hot and is classified as ultra-processed fast food. Nutritionally, the full upsized meal delivers approximately 1030 kcal, 133 g of carbohydrates, 40 g of fat, and 38 g of protein per serving, with very high sodium content (~2015 mg). The meal is certified Halal in Singapore. It is high in refined carbohydrates, saturated fat, and sodium, making it a high-concern meal for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic load per meal is approximately 86.7, placing it in the high glycemic load category despite a medium GI of ~65, due to the large carbohydrate portion size. This meal is commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner option at McDonald's outlets across Singapore and is available on field survey delivery. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those on gluten-free or low-sodium diets.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from McDonald's Singapore published per-item nutrition disclosures combined with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cross-reference for secondary nutrients. The composite per-100g values are calculated estimates from the three meal components. McDonald's Singapore does not publish a full micronutrient panel, so many micronutrient fields are set to -1. GI is an analogous weighted estimate from University of Sydney GI Database component values. No single approved primary source provides exact per-100g data for this composite meal, hence medium confidence.
notes: Composite meal record: the Spicy Chicken McWrap Upsized Meal consists of (1) Spicy Chicken McWrap (~220 g), (2) Large Fries (~154 g), and (3) Medium Soft Drink (~276 g / 250 ml), total ~650 g. Per-100g values are calculated from the composite. Sodium per meal ~2015 mg. Total energy per meal ~1030 kcal. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in McDonald's Singapore nutrition disclosure or the USDA SR Legacy cross-reference used. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in McDonald's Singapore nutrition disclosure. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. water_g is -1 as it is not reported in the source data. field survey source URL preserved from input for provenance traceability but not used as a primary nutrient source per Veritas source policy. Halal certification confirmed per McDonald's Singapore Halal status. The food_id GD000145 is retained from the input seed record.
